eras.name: Dawn of the Space Age
eras.blurb: The Space Race ignites. The first artificial satellite, the first human in orbit, and the first footsteps on the Moon redefine what is possible.

eras.name: Space Station Era
eras.blurb: Humanity learns to live in space. The first orbital stations — Salyut, Skylab and Mir — turn orbit into a place to work, not just visit.

eras.name: Cooperative Era
eras.blurb: The Space Race ends and former rivals build together. The International Space Station partnership lays the first modules of a shared outpost.

eras.name: ISS Assembly Era
eras.blurb: Continuous human presence begins. The ISS grows module by module while the Space Shuttle flies its final missions.

eras.name: Commercial Space Era
eras.blurb: Private companies take the lead. Reusable rockets, commercial cargo, and a wave of CubeSats lower the cost of reaching orbit.

eras.name: Mega-Constellation Age
eras.blurb: Thousands of satellites launch as constellations blanket low Earth orbit, even as Artemis sets its sights back on the Moon.

missions.name: Sputnik 1
missions.description: The first human-made object to orbit Earth. Its simple radio beeps, heard worldwide, opened the Space Age and triggered the Space Race.

missions.name: Explorer 1
missions.description: America's first satellite. Its instruments discovered the Van Allen radiation belts, the first major scientific discovery of the Space Age.

missions.name: Vostok 1 (Gagarin)
missions.description: Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space, completing a single orbit of Earth in 108 minutes.

missions.name: Apollo 11
missions.description: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the Moon while Michael Collins orbited above.

missions.name: Salyut 1
missions.description: The world's first space station. It proved humans could live and work in orbit for extended periods.

missions.name: Skylab
missions.description: America's first space station, hosting three crews who conducted solar astronomy and biology experiments before its 1979 reentry.

missions.name: Mir
missions.description: The first modular space station, assembled in orbit over a decade. Mir held the record for continuous human presence until the ISS.

missions.name: Hubble Space Telescope
missions.description: One of the most productive scientific instruments ever built. Still operational, it continues to reshape our understanding of the cosmos.

missions.name: ISS Zarya Module
missions.description: Zarya was the first module of the International Space Station, providing power and propulsion for the early assembly stage.

missions.name: International Space Station
missions.description: Continuously crewed since November 2000, the ISS is humanity's largest orbital outpost and a model of international cooperation.

missions.name: Envisat
missions.description: A massive Earth-observation satellite that fell silent in 2012. It now drifts as one of the largest pieces of space debris in orbit.

missions.name: STS-135 Atlantis
missions.description: The final flight of the Space Shuttle program, closing a 30-year era and shifting US crew launches toward commercial providers.

missions.name: SpaceX Dragon CRS-1
missions.description: The first contracted commercial resupply mission to the ISS, proving private spacecraft could service the station.

missions.name: Tiangong-1
missions.description: China's first space station prototype, a key step toward the later Tiangong station. It reentered the atmosphere in 2018.

missions.name: Starlink v1.0 L1
missions.description: An early operational Starlink batch. The constellation now numbers thousands of satellites delivering broadband worldwide.

missions.name: Crew Dragon Demo-2
missions.description: The first crewed flight of a commercial spacecraft, returning human launch capability to US soil after the Shuttle's retirement.

missions.name: James Webb Space Telescope
missions.description: Operating a million miles from Earth at the L2 point, JWST observes the earliest galaxies in infrared light.
